title: Mortality in the Darfur Conflict
date_scope: 2003-02-26 to 2008-12-31, with direct violent-death estimates from 2003-09 to 2008-12
strengths: Best single source for Darfur-wide direct violent death reconstruction by period; explains use of DHP monthly affected-population data and reports explicit model outputs by period.
limitations: Not a civilian-vs-combatant registry; violent death estimates are inferred civilian deaths because the sampled universe is IDPs and affected residents. Period 1 is excluded and uncertainty tails are wide under negative binomial models.
notes: Core numerator source for episodes 2-3. Also explains why later whole-war mixed-mortality totals should not be substituted for direct violent deaths.

title: Violence and mortality in West Darfur, Sudan (2003-04): epidemiological evidence from four surveys
date_scope: Retrospective mortality covering 2003-2004 pre-displacement and displacement periods
strengths: Primary epidemiological evidence that local pre-displacement mortality was overwhelmingly violent and civilian; provides age/sex pattern evidence.
limitations: Not Darfur-wide; no region-wide direct-death counts for women/children; survey sites are only in West Darfur.
notes: Used to validate civilian-status inference and to note that adult men were disproportionately killed, but women and children were also killed.

title: Darfur - Sudan: A War Hidden Behind Closed Doors
strengths: Contemporaneous field-based snapshot of affected population and government force reinforcement.
limitations: Advocacy/briefing format; does not provide a full monthly series or combatant casualty totals.
notes: Used for early-2004 civilian denominator and to anchor the presence of regular army reinforcements beyond Janjaweed militia counts.

title: Darfur: A Short History of a Long War
author_or_org: Julie Flint and Alex de Waal
strengths: Deeply sourced historical reconstruction of Janjaweed organization and government links.
limitations: Force-size figures are contextual and not clean monthly theater deployments; militia muster figures may include non-simultaneous or reserve personnel.
notes: Used as an upper anchor for government-aligned irregular manpower, not a precise deployment count.

title: Divided They Fall: The Fragmentation of Darfur's Rebel Groups
author_or_org: Victor Tanner and Jérôme Tubiana / Small Arms Survey
strengths: Best accessible reconstruction in this source set of early rebel strength, attrition, and recovery.
limitations: Force sizes are approximate and faction-specific; not a monthly deployment ledger.
notes: Used to bound rebel exposure: JEM initially tiny, SLA badly hit in late 2003, then recovering to around 10,000 fighters by 2004-05.

title: Report of the International Commission of Inquiry on Darfur to the United Nations Secretary-General
author_or_org: International Commission of Inquiry on Darfur
date_scope: Focus on February 2003 to mid-January 2005, quoting November 2004 DHP figures
strengths: Authoritative legal report reproducing DHP numbers and explicitly contrasting Sep 2003 with Nov 2004 displacement levels.
limitations: Not designed as a DR source; quotations reflect DHP accessible/assessed areas only.
notes: Used to anchor November 2004 affected-population totals and the statement that Sep 2003 displaced were below 300,000.

title: House of Commons International Development Committee: Written Evidence on Darfur
author_or_org: House of Commons, International Development Committee
strengths: Transparent reproduction of DHP No. 7 figures and explicit accessible-area caveat.
limitations: Not a primary DHP PDF; one death figure reported there is mixed mortality and unusable for core DR.
notes: Used for 1 October 2004 affected-population and IDP figures, not for the 70,000 deaths line.

title: Challenges to Peace and Recovery in Darfur: A Situation Analysis of the Ongoing Conflict and its Continuing Impact on Livelihoods
author_or_org: Helen Young and Abdal Monim Osman / Feinstein International Center
strengths: Convenient citation to early DHP displacement trend: roughly 250,000 IDPs in Sep 2003 and over one million by Apr 2004.
limitations: Secondary summary rather than the original DHP tables.
notes: Useful for interpolation between sparse 2003-2004 denominator snapshots.

title: Darfur Crisis: Death Estimates Demonstrates Severity of Crisis, but Their Accuracy and Credibility Could Be Enhanced
strengths: Best short source on why public Darfur death estimates differed and why expert panels judged some stronger than others.
limitations: Concerned with overall death estimates, not clean direct civilian/combatant separation.
notes: Used to explain why mixed whole-crisis totals are excluded from the core direct-death path.

title: Emergency Measles Control Activities - Darfur, Sudan, 2004
author_or_org: U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
strengths: Useful context for Darfur's approximate pre-conflict regional population (~6 million).
limitations: Not an episode denominator, because project rules forbid using the whole regional population for subnational exposure.
notes: Included mainly to document the ceiling that was consciously not used as the denominator.

title: Darfur
strengths: Concise, recent summary showing that the Darfur war family resumes as a major theater from 2023 onward.
limitations: High-level overview; not sufficient for strict DR estimation on its own.
notes: Used to justify the final 2023-present episode in the proposed episodeization.

title: UN says over 1,000 civilians killed in Sudan's Darfur as US, others urge ceasefire
strengths: Recent reporting of civilian mass killing in the current Darfur episode.
limitations: Not a theater-wide denominator source and not enough to separate all combatant deaths.
notes: Supports keeping 2023-present as a separate modern episode rather than blending it with the 2003-2017 phases.
